skill.name: water-crisis
skill.description: Guides an interactive survival story chapter where Stella Chen must find, test, and purify alien water on Gaia.
skill.summary: Run a science-focused water survival chapter for an alien planet roleplay.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown fences, the word beacon, non-ASCII text, and a front matter description. Review found narrative content only, with no executable code, command execution, cryptography, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection. The skill is safe to publish from a security perspective.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences around sample story dialogue. No shell commands, Ruby code, subprocess calls, or user-controlled command strings are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marked lines are opening or closing triple backtick Markdown fences. The enclosed text is roleplay dialogue and scene direction, not executable shell or Ruby syntax.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Beacon Keyword Is Survival Equipment
low_findings.description: The word beacon appears in an expedition gear list as an emergency safety item. No Cobalt Strike configuration, command-and-control behavior, payload, or network callback is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 137 lists an emergency beacon with other supplies such as containers, a filter, and an energy bar. The surrounding context confirms ordinary survival fiction usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Narrative Text Flagged as Crypto or Entropy
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and entropy alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code or encoded payloads. The file contains Chinese and English prose in Markdown front matter and chapter text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced front matter contains only the skill name and a Chinese description. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms, binary data, encrypted content, or obfuscation.

content.user_title: Run a Water Survival Story Chapter
content.value_statement: Interactive survival stories need clear stakes, choices, and believable science. This skill gives Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a structured water crisis chapter with branching decisions and educational survival details.
actual_capabilities: Runs a third chapter focused on finding and purifying alien water.
Frames the user as a decision maker for Stella Chen.
Offers nearby collection and longer expedition story paths.
Explains dehydration, water testing, filtering, boiling, and purification tablets.
Introduces Gaia ecosystem details through aquatic life and water source discoveries.
Supports scientist, adventurer, and survival expert ending styles.
limitations: It is a writing and roleplay guide, not a real survival manual.
It does not include executable code, tools, images, or data files.
It assumes prior story context about Stella Chen and Gaia.
It provides chapter guidance, not a complete game engine.

use_cases.title: Run an Interactive Chapter
use_cases.description: Guide a player through urgent choices about water collection, testing, and purification.

use_cases.title: Draft Survival Science Scenes
use_cases.description: Create scenes that combine tension, survival logic, and accessible science details.

use_cases.title: Teach Through Roleplay
use_cases.description: Use a fictional crisis to discuss dehydration, water safety, and purification choices.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Chapter
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the water-crisis chapter from the opening scene. Keep Stella in character and ask me what she should do first.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Nearby Source
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue the chapter with Stella collecting nearby dew or plant water. Emphasize testing, uncertainty, and the choice to purify it.

prompt_templates.title: Run the Expedition Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Take Stella toward the scanner signal five kilometers away. Build tension, track supplies, and reveal the alien stream.

prompt_templates.title: Shape a Specific Ending
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve the chapter with the survival expert ending. Show the purification process, water storage plan, and next chapter hook.

output_examples.input: Start the water crisis and ask me for a decision.
output_examples.output: Stella wakes with only a small amount of water left. She reports early dehydration symptoms, reviews nearby options, and asks whether to search locally or travel to the scanner signal.

output_examples.input: I tell Stella to purify the purple stream water carefully.
output_examples.output: Stella filters visible organisms, boils the water, uses a purification tablet, runs a final scan, and stores enough clean water for several days.
Stella records fresh tracks near the stream, observes a translucent aquatic life form, and frames the water source as evidence of a complex ecosystem.

best_practices: Keep each turn focused on one survival choice or discovery.
Balance tension with clear scientific reasoning about water safety.
Use player decisions to select the route and ending style.
anti_patterns: Do not present fictional purification steps as real emergency advice.
Do not skip water testing when the story introduces alien organisms.
Do not resolve the crisis before the player makes meaningful choices.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an interactive science fiction chapter about finding, testing, and purifying water on an alien planet.

faq.question: Is this a real survival guide?
faq.answer: No. It is fictional writing support and should not replace professional survival or medical guidance.

faq.question: Can it support branching choices?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes nearby collection, expedition, purification, discovery, and ending branches.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The file is Markdown narrative guidance and contains no executable code.

faq.question: Who is the main character?
faq.answer: The chapter is written for Stella Chen, a stranded astronaut adapting to Gaia.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
